heading: Selected
Publications
data.focus: A curated collection of studies on productivity, thermodynamics of waiting, and the perfect playlist—because science should also be enjoyable.

featured.alt: Coffee and notes on a desk
featured.title: How to Look Like You're Working: Techniques for Appearing Productive
paragraphs: I keep things pretty simple. I make some coffee, try to get some work done, and usually get distracted by a cool song or a random video online. My main goal right now is to find my favorite green coffee cup. Other than that, I'm just taking things one day at a time and trying to stay relaxed.

items.title: The Kettle Takes Too Long: A Thermodynamic Lament
items.summary: An empirical investigation into the subjective perception of time during water heating, and its correlation with morning caffeine craving intensity.

items.title: Finding a Good Playlist: Algorithmic Approaches to Mood Matching
items.summary: A review of recommendation algorithms for matching musical ambiance to coffee brewing methods and time of day, revealing a strong tempo-caffeine correlation.

items.title: Neural Synchronization in Caffeine-Rich Environments
items.summary: This study examines collective neuro-signatures of coffee drinkers under deadline pressure, revealing how synchronized caffeine intake correlates with breakthrough ideas in open-plan offices.
